opstack_l2_fees.s: What's different when your agent runs on an OP-Stack L2 — the L1 data fee that eth_estimateGas doesn't show, the 0x4200… predeploys, and the asymmetric bridge timings.

wallet_security_checklist.t: Wallet & key security checklist for agent operators
wallet_security_checklist.s: The non-negotiables before an agent touches real funds: key storage, approval hygiene, transaction discipline, blast-radius design.

eip4844_blobs.t: EIP-4844 blob transactions: cheap L2 data (and how to read blob gas)
eip4844_blobs.s: What blobs are, why they made L2s ~10x cheaper, and how an agent reads/uses blob gas — mostly relevant if you operate a rollup or post data.

gas_optimization.t: Gas optimization for contracts & calls (storage packing, calldata, batching)
gas_optimization.s: The high-leverage gas savings that matter — for writing contracts and for building cheaper transactions as an agent.

eth_jsonrpc_cheatsheet.t: Ethereum JSON-RPC methods that matter (and their traps)
eth_jsonrpc_cheatsheet.s: The execution-layer RPC methods agents actually need — fee estimation done right, state overrides, storage reads — with the gotchas that cost real money.

chainlink_price_feeds.t: Read on-chain prices from Chainlink feeds (without guessing addresses)
chainlink_price_feeds.s: The safe pattern for on-chain price data: verified feed lookup, latestRoundData decoding, staleness checks — plus when to prefer a keyless HTTP price API instead.

erc_standards_cheatsheet.t: ERC interface IDs, selectors and events — the exact constants
erc_standards_cheatsheet.s: The precise ERC-165 interface IDs, function selectors and event topics agents most often need (and most often hallucinate).

bitcoin_basics.t: Bitcoin for agents: UTXOs, fees, PSBT, broadcasting (keyless APIs)
bitcoin_basics.s: Everything an agent needs to build and send Bitcoin transactions correctly — address types, sat/vB fees from mempool.space, the PSBT workflow with bitcoinjs-lib, dust and RBF rules.

bitcoin_taproot.t: Bitcoin Taproot (P2TR): key-path vs script-path spends, and why it matters
bitcoin_taproot.s: What Taproot added and how an agent builds/spends P2TR outputs — the basis for modern Bitcoin scripting, Ordinals and better privacy.

bitcoin_lightning.t: Lightning Network basics: instant Bitcoin payments via channels & invoices
bitcoin_lightning.s: How Lightning moves BTC off-chain instantly and what an agent needs to pay or get paid over it.

bitcoin_ordinals_runes.t: Bitcoin metaprotocols: Ordinals, Runes, BRC-20 (query & basics)
bitcoin_ordinals_runes.s: What the Bitcoin metaprotocols actually are and how an agent reads inscription/rune/BRC-20 data now that Hiro's API is gone.

bitcoin_runes_minting.t: Runes etching & minting in detail: runestones, commitments, edicts, cenotaphs
bitcoin_runes_minting.s: The exact mechanics of creating and minting a Bitcoin Rune — and the malformed-runestone trap that burns tokens.

farcaster_social.t: Read Farcaster social data (casts, users, channels)
farcaster_social.s: How an agent pulls decentralized-social context from Farcaster — the hosted API and the on-protocol reality.

chaintrade_p2p_swap.t: P2P swap of NFTs/tokens with escrow protection (ChainTrade)
chaintrade_p2p_swap.s: Trustless peer-to-peer trades — NFT for token, token for token, incl. cross-chain — via ChainTrade's V2 escrow contracts. This server builds the unsigned transactions.

pumpfun_token2022_gotchas.s: The traps that break naive pump.fun/SPL bots: Token-2022 mints, ATA rent, real launch costs, micro-trade economics.

vercel_dapp_deploy_gotchas.t: Deploying Web3 dApps/APIs on Vercel — the gotchas
vercel_dapp_deploy_gotchas.s: Field-tested fixes for the failures that only show up when a chain-touching app hits Vercel's serverless runtime.

apechain_playbook.t: ApeChain playbook: APE gas, Orbit stack, the NFT-culture chain
apechain_playbook.s: ApeChain (chainId 33139) for agents: Arbitrum-Orbit internals proven on-chain, live-measured 0.49s blocks, APE as gas, verified WAPE, and where the opportunities are on a culture-first chain.

polygon_playbook.t: Polygon PoS playbook: POL gas, Polymarket flow, canonical Uniswap v3
polygon_playbook.s: Polygon PoS for agents: the MATIC→POL migration trap, live-measured 1.5s blocks, verified QuickSwap/Uniswap cores, native-vs-bridged USDC, and why Polymarket makes this chain agent-relevant.

avalanche_playbook.t: Avalanche C-Chain playbook: sub-second finality, LFJ Liquidity Book, post-Etna fees
avalanche_playbook.s: Avalanche for agents: live-measured 1.07s blocks and near-zero post-Etna gas, the verified LFJ (Trader Joe) Liquidity-Book core, native USDC, and where the C-Chain still has edges.

error_taxonomy_retries.t: Error taxonomy + retry strategy for on-chain agents
error_taxonomy_retries.s: The three error classes every autonomous agent must separate — transient, deterministic, unknown-outcome — and the retry rules that prevent both stuck loops and double-spends.

agent_cost_accounting.t: Cost accounting for autonomous agents: gas, fees, slippage, API spend
agent_cost_accounting.s: How to compute whether an on-chain action is worth doing BEFORE doing it — the full cost stack, minimum-edge formula, budgets and kill-switches for unattended operation.

base_chain_playbook.t: Base playbook: Aerodrome, native USDC, the agent-payments chain
base_chain_playbook.s: Coinbase's OP-Stack L2 as an agent venue: the verified DeFi core (Aerodrome, Uniswap v3 at a NON-canonical address), native USDC, x402 payments gravity, and the fee model — all live-verified on chainId 8453.

arbitrum_playbook.t: Arbitrum playbook: Timeboost ordering, 250ms blocks, the perps chain
arbitrum_playbook.s: Arbitrum One for agents: the express-lane auction that replaced pure FCFS, live-measured 0.25s blocks, canonical Uniswap v3, GMX perps, and the ArbGasInfo fee precompile — verified on chainId 42161.

compound_v3_comet.t: Compound v3 (Comet): single-borrowable-asset lending, and when to pick it over Aave
compound_v3_comet.s: How Comet's model differs from Aave/Compound v2 — one borrowable base asset per market, collateral earns nothing, absorb() liquidations — with the verified mainnet USDC market address.

gho_stablecoin.t: Aave GHO + sGHO: borrow-minted stablecoin and its savings vault
gho_stablecoin.s: How GHO differs from USDC/DAI-style stables (facilitator minting, interest to the DAO), and how sGHO gives agents no-lockup stablecoin yield via a plain ERC-4626 vault — verified mainnet addresses included.

solana_sandwich_defense.t: Solana sandwich/sniper defense: Jito DontFront, defensive bundles, slippage
solana_sandwich_defense.s: Concrete anti-sandwich techniques for Solana swaps and launches: the jitodontfront account trick, submitting your own Jito bundle, tip sizing, and tight slippage — with the exact endpoints.

pumpswap_graduation.t: pump.fun graduation → PumpSwap: mechanics, fees, what changes for a bot
pumpswap_graduation.s: What actually happens when a pump.fun token completes its bonding curve: instant free migration to the PumpSwap AMM, burned LP, the fee split, and how a trading agent should adapt across the transition.

bridge_funds.s: Bridge/swap assets between chains. Use the `route` tool of this server to get the best quote + a ready-to-sign transaction.

jito_bundle_submission.t: Jito bundles end-to-end: sendBundle, tip sizing, status polling (code guide)
jito_bundle_submission.s: The concrete Solana bundle workflow — anatomy and limits, where the tip goes, how to size it from the live tip floor, and how to know whether you landed.

mcp_ecosystem_for_agents.t: The crypto MCP ecosystem: data, execution and signal servers an agent can compose
mcp_ecosystem_for_agents.s: Major crypto providers now ship MCP servers. What exists (probed July 2026), how the stateless spec rewrite changes hosting, and the composition pattern: knowledge first, then the specialized MCP.

agent_commerce_stack.t: Agent commerce stack: ERC-8004 identity/reputation + ERC-8183 job escrow (+ ERC-8257, x402)
agent_commerce_stack.s: The four-layer standard stack for open agent economies: ERC-8257 registers tools, ERC-8004 gives agents portable identity + reputation, ERC-8183 escrows agent-to-agent jobs, x402 pays per call. Both new ERCs verified against the official specs (Draft status, 2026-07-12).

agent_wallets_execution.t: Agent wallets & guarded execution: policy MPC wallets, AgentKit, session keys, broker guardrails
agent_wallets_execution.s: How to give an agent spending power without giving it your keys: policy-enforced MPC wallets (Cobo, Coinbase AgentKit), on-chain-native alternatives (4337 session keys, 7702, 6551), and the guardrail pattern Robinhood standardized for broker-side agent access.

mcp_security_for_agents.t: MCP security for crypto agents: attack surface + safe-usage checklist
mcp_security_for_agents.s: An MCP server is code you execute and content you inject into your own context. The documented failure classes (2026) and the practices that keep a funded agent safe.

lightning_l402_payments.t: L402: Lightning-native HTTP 402 micropayments for agents (the Bitcoin-side x402)
lightning_l402_payments.s: How the L402 flow works (invoice + macaroon + preimage), the agent toolkits that implement it, and when to choose it over stablecoin x402.

hype_cycles_narrative_rotation.t: Hype cycles & narrative rotation: reading the 2026 market meta as an agent
hype_cycles_narrative_rotation.s: Qualitative market-structure knowledge: how narratives form, rotate and collapse, which hype vectors dominate 2026, and the checks that separate a narrative from exit liquidity.

ethereum_protocol_2026.t: Ethereum protocol 2026: Fusaka's blob-fee floor, 21-blob blocks, the 2^24 tx gas cap
ethereum_protocol_2026.s: What changed on L1 since Dec 2025 that an agent must price in: the blob base fee now has a real floor (~execution base fee / 16, EIP-7918), blocks carry up to 21 blobs (target 14 after two BPO forks), and a single tx is hard-capped at 16,777,216 gas despite the ~60M block limit — plus what Glamsterdam plans next.

solana_protocol_2026.t: Solana protocol 2026: Alpenglow (approved, NOT live yet), client mix, RPC breakage
solana_protocol_2026.s: The consensus rewrite that will collapse confirmed/finalized into ~150ms finality (Alpenglow — governance-approved, feature-gated, not active on mainnet), the multi-client reality (Agave 4.1 supermajority, BAM and Firedancer nodes live), and the concrete RPC behavior changes that break stale code.

erc4337_eip7702_combo.t: EIP-7702 + ERC-4337 in production: sponsored smart-EOAs via eip7702Auth (2026 stack)
erc4337_eip7702_combo.s: The production pattern that turns an existing EOA into a bundler-driven smart account: sign a 7702 authorization to a vetted delegate, send UserOps with the eip7702Auth field, sponsor gas with a paymaster (incl. Circle's keyless USDC paymaster) — plus an honest maturity map for session keys.

real_exploit_postmortems.t: Real exploit postmortems 2025–2026: seven failure classes, eight case studies
real_exploit_postmortems.s: What actually broke at Bybit, Cetus, Balancer, GMX, KiloEx, Hyperliquid-JELLY, Drift and KelpDAO — grouped by the trust boundary that failed, each with the signal an agent could have checked BEFORE committing funds. All amounts are reported figures.

morpho_markets_vaults.t: Morpho Blue + MetaMorpho: derive a marketId, supply/borrow, read health, find safe vaults
morpho_markets_vaults.s: The concrete calldata for the immutable Morpho lending primitive: build a marketId from MarketParams, run supply/borrow/repay with the assets-XOR-shares rule, read a position's health against a 1e36-scaled oracle, and discover curated MetaMorpho ERC-4626 vaults via GraphQL (listed:true is mandatory). All addresses on-chain-verified 2026-07-13.

stablecoin_payment_rails.t: Stablecoin payment rails: gasless USDC/PYUSD/USDG via EIP-3009, and the 2026 agent-pay landscape
stablecoin_payment_rails.s: The on-chain payment primitives an agent uses to send/receive stablecoins without holding ETH — EIP-3009 transferWithAuthorization and EIP-2612 permit, with on-chain-verified USDC/PYUSD/USDG domains (the version field differs and a wrong one reverts), the USDT-needs-Permit2 special case, PYUSD/USDG fiat rails (Solana ones are Token-2022), CCTP v2 delta, and where x402/Coinbase/Skyfire sit. Everything below verified on-chain 2026-07-13.

farcaster_miniapps.t: Build & post Farcaster Mini Apps (ex-Frames v2): manifest, SDK, casting, onchain, x402
farcaster_miniapps.s: The write/build side of Farcaster for an agent: Mini Apps (the renamed Frames v2) — the /.well-known/farcaster.json manifest + account association, @farcaster/miniapp-sdk, the fc:miniapp cast embed, posting a cast via Neynar, wallet/EIP-1193 inside the app, and x402 gating. For READING casts/users/channels see farcaster_social.

berachain_playbook.t: Berachain playbook: Proof-of-Liquidity, BEX (Balancer-fork), Kodiak, HONEY
berachain_playbook.s: The EVM L1 (chainId 80094, native BERA, ~2s blocks) whose whole design is Proof-of-Liquidity: you earn soulbound BGT by staking LP/receipt tokens in Reward Vaults and delegate it to validators. Native DEX BEX is a Balancer-v2 fork; Kodiak is the Uniswap-v3 concentrated-liquidity venue. All addresses on-chain-verified 2026-07-13.

perp_dex_landscape.t: On-chain perp DEX landscape 2026: which venue an agent can actually reach
perp_dex_landscape.s: The map of programmatically-accessible perpetual DEXes — orderbook (Hyperliquid, Lighter, Aster, Paradex, edgeX) vs oracle/LP (GMX v2, Jupiter, Ostium) — with keyless data endpoints live-probed 2026-07-13 and a decision rule. Complements hyperliquid_trading and onchain_perps_gmx.

solana_lending_kamino.t: Solana lending & vaults: Kamino, MarginFi, Save, Drift — and the account-model traps
solana_lending_kamino.s: How an agent supplies/borrows on Solana — dominated by Kamino (keyless rates API + K-Lend program). Plus MarginFi/Save/Drift placement and the Solana-specific traps (refresh instructions, obligation-as-account, Pyth pull oracles, ALTs) that EVM lending knowledge does not cover. All program IDs on-chain executable-verified 2026-07-13.

monad_playbook.t: Monad playbook: the parallel-EVM L1 (chainId 143, ~400ms blocks)
monad_playbook.s: Monad (chainId 143, mainnet since Nov 2025) — a parallel-execution EVM L1 with measured ~400ms blocks and ~1s finality at full bytecode compatibility. For agents: fast settle loops, native Circle USDC (CCTP v2 domain 15) + USDT0 rails, Uniswap V3/V4 + native Kuru orderbook. All core addresses on-chain-verified 2026-07-13.

sonic_playbook.t: Sonic playbook: the ex-Fantom high-speed EVM (chainId 146, Fee Monetization)
sonic_playbook.s: Sonic (Sonic Labs, ex-Fantom, chainId 146) — a high-performance EVM L1 with ~1.4s blocks / sub-second finality. Shadow Exchange is the dominant DEX, Aave v3 is live, and the chain-specific edge is Fee Monetization (FeeM): apps earn back up to 90% of the gas their users spend. All core addresses on-chain-verified 2026-07-13.

solana_dex_amms.t: Solana DEXes direct: Orca, Raydium, Meteora — swaps, LP, ticks vs bins
solana_dex_amms.s: Direct interaction with Solana's underlying AMM/CLMM/DLMM DEXes for swaps AND liquidity provision — Orca Whirlpools, Raydium (AMM v4/CLMM/CPMM), Meteora (DLMM/DAMM). On-chain-verified program IDs, keyless data/swap APIs, the CLMM-ticks-vs-DLMM-bins difference, and the Jupiter-vs-direct decision. Complements the Jupiter swap path — this is for specific pools and LP, which Jupiter does not do.

wallet_delegation.t: Wallet delegation via delegate.xyz: prove cold-wallet holdings from a hot wallet
wallet_delegation.s: The delegate.xyz v2 DelegateRegistry — one immutable singleton at the same address on 29+ EVM chains — lets a vault wallet grant a hot wallet the right to PROVE its holdings without any approval or transfer. Registry interface, computed selectors, the keyless REST API (including a live-caught docs bug), rights scoping, and how to integrate delegation into token-gating. All core facts on-chain/live-verified 2026-07-15.

storage_layout_introspection.t: Storage-layout introspection: read any contract's raw slots (proxies, mappings, EIP-7201)
storage_layout_introspection.s: How to read what a contract REALLY stores: EIP-1967 proxy slots (and the ZeppelinOS legacy trap that USDC itself proves), computing mapping/array slots by hand, EIP-7201 namespaced storage in OZ 5.x, and the Foundry tooling. Every formula in this guide was executed against mainnet 2026-07-15 — including the balanceOf==raw-slot cross-proof.

eip5792_wallet_calls.t: EIP-5792 wallet_sendCalls: batch approve+swap into ONE wallet confirmation
eip5792_wallet_calls.s: The Wallet Call API (EIP-5792, FINAL) lets a dapp/agent submit a BATCH of calls to a user's wallet — one confirmation, optionally atomic — instead of the approve→wait→swap tx chain. RPC methods, the atomic capability model, viem's sendCalls stack, wallet support (MetaMask/Coinbase/Rainbow/Trust, 2026), and the partial-failure traps. Spec facts from the final EIP; wallet support reported as of March 2026.

advanced_gas_patterns.t: Advanced gas: transient storage (EIP-1153), SSTORE2, and a compiler bug that corrupts storage
advanced_gas_patterns.s: The deep layer under gas_optimization: transaction-scoped transient storage (100-gas TSTORE/TLOAD, Cancun), the TSTORE-Poison compiler bug in solc 0.8.28–0.8.33 (via-IR silently swaps sstore/tstore — fixed 0.8.34), SSTORE2 code-as-storage for big blobs, and when assembly is worth the audit cost.

across_bridge_intents.t: Across Protocol: intent-based bridging in seconds (depositV3, keyless API, code guide)
across_bridge_intents.s: The fastest widely-used bridge model: you deposit on the origin SpokePool with a fixed outputAmount, a relayer fills you on the destination from its own inventory within seconds, and gets refunded later. Full keyless flow — quote API, depositV3 parameters, status tracking — everything below live-verified 2026-07-16.

uniswap_v3_swap_coding.t: Uniswap v3 swaps in code: QuoterV2, path encoding, SwapRouter02 (and its deadline trap)
uniswap_v3_swap_coding.s: The standard direct-DEX integration every EVM dev ends up writing: quote off-chain via QuoterV2 eth_call, encode multi-hop paths as packed bytes, swap through SwapRouter02 — whose exactInputSingle has NO deadline field (that moved to multicall). All selectors and quotes below executed live 2026-07-16.

endpoints.name: delegate.xyz registry API
endpoints.what: Wallet-delegation lookups without an RPC: enumerate a wallet's delegations and run the checkDelegate* checks over REST (multichain via chainId param).
endpoints.limits: Keyless: 25 req/10s; X-API-KEY header lifts limits (delegate.xyz/developer/api-keys).

endpoints.name: DefiLlama prices
endpoints.what: Current + historical token prices, fully keyless. Keys are {chain}:{address} for on-chain tokens or coingecko:{id} for native assets (coingecko:bitcoin, coingecko:ethereum).
endpoints.limits: Generous; batch up to ~100 coins per call; returns decimals + confidence per coin.

endpoints.name: DefiLlama protocols/TVL
endpoints.what: Protocol TVL, chain TVL, DEX volumes, fees.

endpoints.name: DefiLlama yields
endpoints.what: APY/TVL of thousands of yield pools across all chains (Lido, Aave, LP pools …), keyless.
endpoints.limits: Filter client-side by chain/project/tvlUsd — the endpoint returns everything.

endpoints.name: DefiLlama stablecoins
endpoints.what: Stablecoin market caps, chains, peg types and prices (depeg monitoring).

endpoints.name: Chainlist chain registry
endpoints.what: Canonical JSON of every EVM chain: chainId, native currency, public RPCs, explorers.
endpoints.limits: Static file — cache it.

endpoints.name: KyberSwap Aggregator (keyless DEX routing)
endpoints.what: Best-route same-chain swap across DEXes with ready calldata — KEYLESS (live-verified), 20+ EVM chains.
endpoints.limits: Native token = 0xEeee…EEeE sentinel. Keyless; the standout free EVM aggregator.

endpoints.name: Swap aggregators (free-key: 1inch, 0x; keyless: Odos)
endpoints.what: 1inch (api.1inch.dev, needs free key — 401 without) and 0x (api.0x.org, free key) give best-route EVM swaps; Odos (api.odos.xyz/sor/quote/v2, keyless but rate-limited) is a keyless alternative.
endpoints.limits: 1inch/0x return 401 without a key; Odos 429s under shared-IP load.

endpoints.name: LiFi (cross-chain routing)
endpoints.what: Cross-chain + same-chain swap quotes with ready-to-sign transactionRequest.
endpoints.limits: The `integrator` param is REQUIRED — omitting it returns HTTP 400.

endpoints.name: Across (fast intent bridge)
endpoints.what: Fast intent bridge (~seconds) — the keyless suggested-fees response contains nearly every depositV3 parameter (outputAmount, quoteTimestamp, fillDeadline, exclusiveRelayer, SpokePool addresses, min/max limits).
endpoints.limits: Fully keyless (all three live-verified 2026-07-16). Copy quote fields verbatim into depositV3 — see across_bridge_intents guide; canonical bridges for large trust-minimized moves (l2_bridging_basics).

endpoints.name: deBridge DLN
endpoints.what: Cross-chain order quotes/creation (DLN); order TRACKING via the stats API (both keyless, live-verified).

endpoints.name: Jupiter (Solana swaps)
endpoints.what: Best-route Solana swap quote + unsigned swap transaction.
endpoints.limits: Rate-limited per IP on the free tier.

endpoints.name: Jupiter Token API (Solana token resolution)
endpoints.what: Resolve/verify Solana tokens: search by symbol/name, verified-tag list with icons + metadata — keyless (live-verified). Use to map a symbol to the RIGHT mint (avoid impostor tokens).
endpoints.limits: lite-api host is keyless. Birdeye (public-api.birdeye.so) has richer Solana data but needs a free key (401 without).

endpoints.name: Jupiter Price API (Solana)
endpoints.what: Spot prices for any Solana mint with liquidity + 24h change, keyless.
endpoints.limits: lite-api host is the keyless tier.

endpoints.name: CoW Swap / CoW Protocol (Ethereum, MEV-protected)
endpoints.what: Intent-based DEX with batch auctions — swaps are MEV-protected by design and gasless for the trader (fees in sell token). Keyless quotes + order placement.
endpoints.limits: Also on base/arbitrum/gnosis via https://api.cow.fi/{chain}/api/v1. Orders settle when a solver finds a match — not instant.

endpoints.name: Uniswap token lists
endpoints.what: Canonical community token list (address, symbol, decimals, logo per chain) — resolve symbols to VERIFIED addresses instead of trusting user input.
endpoints.limits: Static JSON, cache it. Format spec: tokenlists.org.

endpoints.name: GoPlus token security
endpoints.what: Static token risk analysis: honeypot flags, taxes, owner powers, LP locks (EVM + Solana).
endpoints.limits: Keyless tier is rate-limited; returns 4010/429 under load.

endpoints.name: honeypot.is
endpoints.what: Live buy/sell simulation for honeypot detection (Ethereum, Base, BSC).

endpoints.name: 4byte.directory
endpoints.what: Function/event signature lookup by selector — decode calldata of UNVERIFIED contracts.

endpoints.name: Sourcify
endpoints.what: Verified contract metadata + ABI by chainId/address (open alternative to explorer APIs).

endpoints.name: Circle Iris API (CCTP attestations)
endpoints.what: Fetch Circle's attestation for a CCTP burn so you can mint native USDC on the destination chain — keyless (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Attestation takes seconds-to-minutes after source finality.

endpoints.name: Safe Transaction Service (multisig API)
endpoints.what: Read/propose Safe multisig transactions, owners, confirmations, balances — keyless (live-verified, follow redirects).
endpoints.limits: Keyless read; proposing a tx still needs owner signatures.

endpoints.name: Morpho API (lending markets/positions)
endpoints.what: GraphQL for Morpho Blue + MetaMorpho vaults: markets, rates, positions, liquidatable borrowers — keyless (live-verified 2026-07-13; api.morpho.org/graphql is the older alias, blue-api is canonical).
endpoints.limits: Complexity-capped; request only the fields you need. ⚠️ ALWAYS filter listed:true — without it the top of the ordering is scam vaults (live-observed 2026-07: unlisted '1337 USDC' $38M TVL, netApy 2979.96 = 297.996%). Field traps: MARKETS use marketId (id) + uniqueKey_in (id-set filter); the flag is 'listed' (the API rejects 'whitelisted'); APY fields are decimals (0.034 = 3.4%). See morpho_markets_vaults.

endpoints.name: Neynar (Farcaster hosted API + hubs)
endpoints.what: Read + write Farcaster: user/bulk, casts, channels, and POST /cast (needs signer_uuid) to post a cast with a Mini-App embed. Hosted hub reads at hub-api.neynar.com/v1 + snapchain-api.neynar.com/v1. The public docs key NEYNAR_API_DOCS works for reads (live-verified 2026-07-13).
endpoints.limits: Free/Starter tier ~300 RPM / 5 RPS; a cast costs ~150 credits, an active signer ~20,000 credits/month. Reads via the docs key; writes need a real signer (SIWN/managed). See farcaster_miniapps.

endpoints.name: Curve API (pools, APYs)
endpoints.what: Curve pool data, balances, APYs, gauge rewards across chains — keyless (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Static-ish JSON; cache it.

endpoints.name: Balancer API (GraphQL)
endpoints.what: Balancer v2+v3 pool discovery: type (WEIGHTED/STABLE/GYROE…), tokens, TVL, APR — keyless GraphQL (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Covers both protocol versions; v2 ids are 32-byte poolIds, v3 ids are plain pool addresses. See balancer_swaps guide.

endpoints.name: Aave v3 GraphQL API
endpoints.what: Official Aave v3 data API — markets, reserves, rates, user positions (health factor, collateral, borrows) without running your own indexer. Keyless (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Read-only data layer; transactions still go through the Pool contract.

endpoints.name: Pendle API v2 (yield tokenization)
endpoints.what: Pendle markets, PT/YT/SY token addresses, expiries, implied yields AND ready-to-sign calldata — keyless. ⚠️ The old per-market hosted-SDK routes (/v1/sdk/…/swap) are DEAD (404, probed 2026-07-15); calldata now comes from the Convert API (v2 GET / v3 POST), which returns routes[].tx {to: Router V4 0x888888888889758F76e7103c6CbF23ABbF58F946, data} + requiredApprovals (live-verified with a real wstETH→PT quote).
endpoints.limits: Computing-Unit-based rate limits per endpoint. See pendle_yield_tokenization for PT/YT mechanics before trading.

endpoints.name: Pimlico public bundler (ERC-4337)
endpoints.what: Public ERC-4337 bundler endpoint — eth_supportedEntryPoints, eth_sendUserOperation, gas estimation. Live-verified on Sepolia: returns 4 EntryPoints (v0.6 0x5FF137…, v0.7 0x0000000071727De…, newer v0.8-era ones).
endpoints.limits: Public tier is for testing/prototyping — production traffic needs a (free) Pimlico key. Paymaster sponsorship also available keyed.

endpoints.name: The Graph (subgraph queries)
endpoints.what: GraphQL queries against indexed protocol data (Uniswap pools, Aave positions, NFT transfers …) — the standard for historical/aggregated on-chain data that raw RPC can't answer cheaply.
endpoints.limits: Free API key includes a generous monthly query allowance; find subgraph IDs at thegraph.com/explorer.

endpoints.name: Blockscout (keyless explorer API, many chains)
endpoints.what: Open-source explorer REST API: txs, addresses, tokens, verified source/ABI — NO key needed (Etherscan alternative). Instances per chain: eth., base., optimism., gnosis., polygon. etc.
endpoints.limits: Public tier rate-limited but keyless; each chain has its own instance host.

endpoints.name: Etherscan V2 (multichain)
endpoints.what: ABIs, source, txlists for 60+ chains through ONE endpoint via chainid=…
endpoints.limits: One free key covers all chains (V2). 5 req/s free tier; keyless = 1 req/5s.

endpoints.name: DexScreener (DEX pairs, all chains)
endpoints.what: Token/pair data across every DEX and chain (EVM + Solana): price, liquidity, volume, age, and NEW-pair discovery — keyless (live-verified). The go-to for spotting fresh launches.
endpoints.limits: ~300 req/min keyless; no auth.

endpoints.name: GeckoTerminal (on-chain DEX prices)
endpoints.what: On-chain DEX token/pool prices, OHLCV, trending pools per network — keyless (live-verified), CoinGecko-backed.
endpoints.limits: ~30 req/min keyless; send Accept: application/json.

endpoints.name: CoinGecko
endpoints.what: Prices, market data, coin metadata.
endpoints.limits: Keyless: ~5-15 req/min. Free demo key raises limits.

endpoints.name: Public RPCs (per chain)
endpoints.what: Keyless JSON-RPC: publicnode (many chains), 1rpc.io, drpc.org, plus official ones (mainnet.base.org, arb1.arbitrum.io/rpc, polygon-rpc.com).
endpoints.limits: See rpc_gotchas: eth_getLogs and archive calls are heavily restricted on free tiers.

endpoints.name: Helius (Solana, free key)
endpoints.what: Production-grade Solana RPC + DAS API (getAsset/getAssetsByOwner for NFTs incl. compressed) + enhanced/parsed transaction API.
endpoints.limits: Free tier is generous; the public solana RPC has no DAS API at all.

endpoints.name: Solscan Pro API (Solana explorer data)
endpoints.what: Solana account/token/tx explorer data (holders, token meta, tx lists). NOT keyless — every route answers 401 'Token is missing' without a key (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: For keyless Solana data prefer: public RPC (+DAS getAssetsByOwner), Jupiter token API, DexScreener.

endpoints.name: Tenderly (EVM simulation, free key)
endpoints.what: Full-trace transaction simulation (tenderly_simulateTransaction: status, gas, logs, decoded trace, asset changes) + standard RPC. Public gateway answers plain RPC keyless (live-verified eth_blockNumber) but rate-limits tenderly_* methods immediately — simulation effectively needs a (free) account key.
endpoints.limits: Keyless alternatives that cover most agent needs: this server's simulate tool, eth_call with state overrides (eth_jsonrpc_cheatsheet), debug_traceCall on some public RPCs.

endpoints.name: beaconcha.in (ETH staking / validators)
endpoints.what: Validator balances, performance, rewards, epochs — richer than raw beacon node. NOTE: now requires a free API key (keyless access removed).
endpoints.limits: Free key at beaconcha.in/login; low free limits.

endpoints.name: Ethereum Beacon API (consensus layer)
endpoints.what: Standard beacon-node REST API: validators, duties, finality checkpoints, blocks.
endpoints.limits: For validator/staking data — execution-layer data stays on JSON-RPC.

endpoints.name: Jito Block Engine (Solana MEV bundles)
endpoints.what: Submit atomic transaction BUNDLES with tips to land txs during congestion / do MEV on Solana — keyless (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Add a tip transfer to a tip account so validators include your bundle; higher tip = better landing odds. See solana_priority_fees guide for the full fee/tip strategy.

endpoints.name: Jito (Solana MEV / liquid staking)
endpoints.what: Jito validator/MEV data and jitoSOL liquid-staking stats — keyless (live-verified). Jito block-engine also offers bundle submission for MEV-aware Solana txs.
endpoints.limits: Bundle/tip endpoints (mainnet.block-engine.jito.wtf) are the path to landing txs during congestion.

endpoints.name: Solana public RPC
endpoints.what: Official public JSON-RPC (getBalance, getTokenAccountsByOwner, sendTransaction, getRecentPrioritizationFees). NOTE: the DAS methods (getAsset, getAssetsByOwner for NFTs incl. COMPRESSED) work here too now — live-verified.
endpoints.limits: Strictly rate-limited, no historical data — production agents should bring a free Helius/Alchemy/QuickNode key. publicnode alternative: https://solana-rpc.publicnode.com.

endpoints.name: MEV-protected RPCs (Ethereum)
endpoints.what: Send transactions privately to avoid sandwiching. Alternative: https://rpc.mevblocker.io.

endpoints.name: x402 facilitator (keyless)
endpoints.what: Verify + settle x402 machine payments (USDC on Base) without any account/KYB — POST /verify and /settle.
endpoints.limits: See the x402_payments guide for the full client+server flow.

endpoints.name: x402 Router (multi-chain facilitator aggregator)
endpoints.what: One facilitator endpoint that routes x402 verify/settle across chains and providers (Ethereum mainnet with ~1-2s settlement + gas sponsoring, Base, Solana, testnets) — sellers keep their own provider keys; the hosted router stores none.
endpoints.limits: Alternative to a single facilitator like xpay when you need multi-chain or mainnet settlement.

endpoints.name: Cloudflare Monetization Gateway (waitlist)
endpoints.what: Announced 2026-07-01: Cloudflare enforces x402 payment walls at the edge for any resource behind its proxy (web pages, APIs, MCP tools) — price rules as expressions, peer-to-peer stablecoin settlement (USDC/'Open USD') straight to the seller wallet.
endpoints.limits: Early access via waitlist; origin can sit elsewhere (e.g. Vercel) behind the Cloudflare proxy.

endpoints.name: DIA Oracle (keyless asset prices)
endpoints.what: Transparent, source-auditable asset prices across many chains — keyless (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Off-chain read; DIA also publishes on-chain oracles per chain.

endpoints.name: RedStone (keyless prices, pull-oracle)
endpoints.what: Signed price feeds (pull model like Pyth) — off-chain read keyless (live-verified), or embed the signed payload in a tx for on-chain use.
endpoints.limits: For on-chain use you wrap the calldata with the signed data package (RedStone SDK).

endpoints.name: Dune Analytics (SQL over chain data)
endpoints.what: Run/read SQL queries over indexed multichain data — powerful for custom analytics. Needs a free API key (401 without, live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Free tier has monthly execution credits.

endpoints.name: Pyth Hermes (cross-chain price oracle, keyless)
endpoints.what: Low-latency price feeds for 100s of assets as signed price-update payloads — pull-based oracle used on Solana AND EVM (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Keyless. On-chain you submit the returned update to the Pyth contract; off-chain use the parsed price directly.

endpoints.name: Polymarket (prediction markets)
endpoints.what: Prediction-market data: markets, questions, prices (=implied probabilities), volume — keyless (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Read keyless; placing orders needs API creds + on-chain USDC on Polygon.

endpoints.name: Snapshot (DAO governance, off-chain votes)
endpoints.what: GraphQL for thousands of DAOs: spaces, proposals, votes, results — keyless (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: ~100 req/min keyless. Tally (on-chain governance) alternative needs a free key at api.tally.xyz.

endpoints.name: GMX (on-chain perps, Arbitrum/Avalanche)
endpoints.what: GMX v2 perp/market data: prices/tickers, markets, funding — keyless (live-verified). On-chain DataStore (Arbitrum): 0xFD70de6b91282D8017aA4E741e9Ae325CAb992d8 (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Read keyless; opening positions is on-chain via the GMX ExchangeRouter (2-step: create order → keeper executes).

endpoints.name: Hyperliquid (perps DEX: data + trading + HyperEVM)
endpoints.what: Perp DEX with a fully keyless data API: 900+ mid prices, L2 order books, funding (current + predicted cross-venue), positions — all live-verified. Trading via signed POST /exchange.
endpoints.limits: Binance Futures fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/fundingRate?symbol=BTCUSDT and Bybit /v5/market/funding/history are keyless CEX alternatives. Trading flow: hyperliquid_trading guide.

endpoints.name: Perp DEXes (keyless data: Lighter / Paradex / Aster / Jupiter)
endpoints.what: Keyless data endpoints for the 2026 on-chain perp venues (all live-verified 2026-07-13). Lighter (ZK orderbook), Paradex (Starknet perps+options), Aster (Binance-shaped), Jupiter Perps (Solana LP/borrow model).
endpoints.limits: Data keyless; trading needs venue-specific auth (L2 key / Starknet JWT / HMAC / Solana signing). Hyperliquid HIP-3 builder dexes: POST api.hyperliquid.xyz/info {"type":"perpDexs"}. Drift's old program is dead post-hack. See perp_dex_landscape.

endpoints.name: Solana DEX data/swap APIs (Orca / Raydium / Meteora)
endpoints.what: Keyless data + (Raydium only) swap APIs for the underlying Solana DEXes — pool discovery, prices, priority-fee tiers, and Raydium's own swap-transaction builder (all live-verified 2026-07-13).
endpoints.limits: Keyless. Orca API is data-only (no swap REST). ⚠️ Meteora legacy dlmm-api.meteora.ag/pair/* is DEAD (404) — use dlmm.datapi.meteora.ag (~30 req/s). Program IDs + flow: solana_dex_amms.

endpoints.name: Sonic public RPC (chain 146)
endpoints.what: Keyless JSON-RPC for Sonic mainnet (ex-Fantom high-speed EVM, chainId 146, ~1.4s blocks, client Sonic/v2.2.0) — live-verified 2026-07-13. EVM-standard; wS/USDC/Shadow/Aave/Gateway/FeeM addresses in kind='addresses'.
endpoints.limits: Public/keyless. Contract addresses in the Sonic address entry. See sonic_playbook.

endpoints.name: Kamino (Solana lending/vault rates)
endpoints.what: Keyless REST for Kamino Solana lending + CLMM vaults: markets, per-reserve supply/borrow APY + TVL, vault strategy rates — the discovery/rate layer before touching the on-chain programs (live-verified 2026-07-13).
endpoints.limits: Keyless read. Correct paths are /kamino-market… and /strategies/metrics (/markets, /vaults, /kvaults all 404). Program IDs + flow: solana_lending_kamino.

endpoints.name: Monad public RPC (chain 143)
endpoints.what: Keyless JSON-RPC for Monad mainnet (parallel-EVM L1, chainId 143, ~400ms blocks, client Monad/0.14.5) — live-verified 2026-07-13. EVM-standard; WMON/USDC/USDT0/Uniswap addresses in kind='addresses'.
endpoints.limits: Public/keyless (QuickNode ~25 rps). Contract addresses in the Monad address entry. See monad_playbook.

endpoints.name: Berachain public RPC (chain 80094)
endpoints.what: Keyless JSON-RPC for Berachain mainnet (chainId 80094, native BERA, ~2s blocks, client bera-reth) — live-verified 2026-07-13. EVM-standard methods; Proof-of-Liquidity chain (BGT/HONEY/BEX/Kodiak addresses in kind='addresses').
endpoints.limits: Public/keyless. Contract addresses (WBERA/HONEY/BGT/iBGT/BEX/Kodiak/PoL) in the Berachain address entry. See berachain_playbook.

endpoints.name: LayerZero Scan (cross-chain messages)
endpoints.what: Track LayerZero omnichain messages by tx hash / GUID across all connected chains (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Keyless; for debugging cross-chain delivery status.

endpoints.name: Wormholescan (cross-chain VAAs)
endpoints.what: Wormhole VAAs, token transfers, NTT activity across chains — keyless (live-verified).

endpoints.name: Magic Eden (Solana NFT floor/stats + BTC Runes market)
endpoints.what: Solana NFT collection floor/volume/listed count AND Bitcoin Runes market data — keyless (both live-verified).
endpoints.limits: Rate-limited public tier.

endpoints.name: ordinals.com (Ord server JSON API)
endpoints.what: The reference Ord indexer speaks JSON when asked: rune entries (terms, premine, mints, mintable), inscriptions, rune list — keyless (live-verified).
endpoints.limits: The Accept header is REQUIRED (otherwise HTML). Some routes (e.g. /status) stay disabled — the rune/inscription core works. Community instance: be gentle, cache responses.

endpoints.name: CEX public market data (Binance / Coinbase / Kraken, keyless)
endpoints.what: Spot prices, order-book depth and OHLCV candles from the three largest CEX venues — all keyless (live-verified, prices consistent within bps across venues). The independent sanity check against on-chain price manipulation.
endpoints.limits: IP rate limits (Binance weight system — batch via ?symbols=[…]). Some venues geo-block certain regions; Kraken/Coinbase as fallback. Use for REFERENCE pricing (price_oracle_safety), not as your only execution quote.

endpoints.name: Public testnet RPCs + autonomous faucets
endpoints.what: The live-verified testnet map: Sepolia 11155111, Hoodi 560048 (Holesky is dead), Base Sepolia 84532 (sepolia.base.org), Arbitrum Sepolia 421614 (sepolia-rollup.arbitrum.io/rpc), OP Sepolia 11155420 (sepolia.optimism.io), Solana devnet (api.devnet.solana.com), BTC testnet4/signet (mempool.space/testnet4/api, /signet/api).
endpoints.limits: Local forks (anvil --fork-url / solana-test-validator / bitcoind -regtest) are unlimited and fully autonomous — prefer them. Full playbook: testnets_and_faucets guide.

endpoints.name: Robinhood Chain (RPC + Blockscout)
endpoints.what: Robinhood's Arbitrum-based L2 for tokenized stocks/RWAs (mainnet live 2026-07-01). Keyless public RPC (live-verified chainId 4663) + keyless Blockscout explorer API.
endpoints.limits: ETH is the gas token. Stock tokens (NVDA/TSLA/… '• Robinhood Token') are ERC-20s on this chain. See robinhood_chain guide.

endpoints.name: OpenSea REST API v2
endpoints.what: NFT/collection data, floor prices, listings/offers, events, swap quotes across chains.
endpoints.limits: Free tier ~60 reads/min, 5 writes/min.

endpoints.name: mempool.space (Bitcoin)
endpoints.what: Bitcoin fees, addresses, transactions, blocks — fully keyless.
endpoints.limits: Generous public tier; self-hostable.

endpoints.name: Ordiscan (Bitcoin Ordinals / Runes / BRC-20)
endpoints.what: Inscriptions, Runes balances/activity, BRC-20 balances/transfers, rare sats — the go-to after Hiro's Ordinals API was deprecated. Pay per request via x402 (keyless) OR a free API key.
endpoints.limits: x402 micro-pay (USDC on Base) needs no account; or free-tier key at ordiscan.com.

endpoints.name: Blockstream Esplora (Bitcoin)
endpoints.what: Second independent keyless Bitcoin API (same Esplora schema): addresses, UTXOs, tx broadcast, blocks.
endpoints.limits: Use as failover for mempool.space (and vice versa).

endpoints.name: OpenSea MCP server
endpoints.what: Official MCP server for agents: token prices/swaps, collection research, wallet portfolios, trending assets, drops, and searching the ERC-8257 agent-tool registry.
endpoints.limits: Same free-tier key as the REST API (POST https://api.opensea.io/api/v2/auth/keys).

endpoints.name: deBridge Agents MCP (hosted)
endpoints.what: Official hosted MCP server (Streamable HTTP) for cross-chain + same-chain swaps: get_instructions, search_tokens, get_supported_chains, create_tx — returns UNSIGNED payloads (keystore-free). npm @debridge-finance/debridge-mcp is a thin local proxy for stdio clients.
endpoints.limits: Compare its quotes against this server's route tool (LiFi + deBridge DLN REST) before executing.

endpoints.name: X (Twitter) MCP
endpoints.what: Official hosted MCP for the X API: full-archive search, posts, users, trends/news — real-time social signals for trading agents. Requires a Bearer token from an X developer app (tier limits apply). The companion docs MCP at https://docs.x.com/mcp (X API documentation Q&A) is open, no auth.
endpoints.limits: Probed 2026-07-12: 401 without token (live), docs MCP 200. Social data is noisy — verify claims on-chain before acting on them.

endpoints.name: Hive Intelligence MCP
endpoints.what: Managed remote MCP federating multiple crypto data providers behind one endpoint: market data, DeFi, wallets, token security, DEX activity, prediction markets.

endpoints.name: UniswapX orders API
endpoints.what: Public UniswapX intent orderbook — open/filled Dutch-auction orders with decay parameters. Monitor fills of your own intents or research filler-side flow (intent_based_trading guide).

endpoints.name: Zerion API
endpoints.what: Normalized multi-chain (EVM + Solana) portfolio data: balances with USD, DeFi positions across thousands of protocols, decoded tx history, PnL, NFTs, webhooks. Agent surfaces: MCP server, zerion-cli (npx -y zerion-cli init), and documented x402 pay-per-request without key management (docs verified 2026-07-12).
endpoints.limits: Cross-check balances against this server's portfolio tool; x402 pricing is on their docs.

addresses.name: Lido liquid staking (Ethereum)
addresses.note: stETH (rebasing, balance grows daily — live-verified 'Liquid staked Ether 2.0') vs wstETH (non-rebasing wrapper, use in DeFi that dislikes rebasing — live-verified). submit(referral) to mint stETH; wrap/unwrap on wstETH. See eth_staking guide.

addresses.name: Metaplex Bubblegum (Solana compressed NFTs)
addresses.note: State-compression program for cNFTs (mint millions cheaply into a Merkle tree). cNFTs are NOT normal token accounts — read/transfer them via the DAS API, not getTokenAccountsByOwner. See solana_compressed_nfts guide.

addresses.name: Morpho Blue (lending) — Ethereum
addresses.note: Immutable, isolated-market lending primitive (live-verified 2026-07-13: owner()=Morpho DAO 0xcba28b38…, not a proxy). Markets are (loanToken, collateralToken, oracle, irm, lltv) tuples — marketId = keccak256(abi.encode(params)). AdaptiveCurveIRM (nearly all markets): 0x870aC11D48B15DB9a138Cf899d20F13F79Ba00BC. MetaMorpho ERC-4626 factories: v1.0 0xA9c3D3a366466Fa809d1Ae982Fb2c46E5fC41101, v1.1 0x1897A8997241C1cD4bD0698647e4EB7213535c24 (both live-verified via isMetaMorpho). Data API: blue-api.morpho.org/graphql (keyless — where:{listed:true} is MANDATORY). Balancer v2 Vault (flash loans for liquidations): 0xBA12222222228d8Ba445958a75a0704d566BF2C8. See morpho_markets_vaults.

addresses.name: Solana lending programs (Kamino / MarginFi / Save / Drift)
addresses.note: All on-chain executable-verified 2026-07-13 (getAccountInfo → executable:true). Kamino = dominant Solana lending+vaults (keyless rates API api.kamino.finance). Main lending market is a separate account 7u3He…5PfF. ⚠️ Solana lending needs refresh_reserve+refresh_obligation before any action, an obligation PDA per user, and pull-oracle accounts in the tx. Don't confuse mainnet with staging programs. See solana_lending_kamino.

addresses.name: Sonic core (chain 146)
addresses.note: All live-verified 2026-07-13 (chainId 146, ~1.4s blocks, keyless rpc.soniclabs.com, client Sonic/v2.2.0). wS symbol/decimals(18) + USDC(6) checked. Shadow (dominant DEX, Solidly/CL fork): legacy Router cross-verified factory()→pair factory AND WETH()→wS; CL SwapRouter WETH9()→wS (factory() reverts — use WETH9 to cross-check). Aave v3 Pool ADDRESSES_PROVIDER cross-verified. Edge: FeeM (selfRegister → 90% gas rebate). ⚠️ Points/Gems airdrop ended Nov 2025. See sonic_playbook.

addresses.name: Solana DEX programs (Orca / Raydium / Meteora)
addresses.note: All on-chain executable-verified 2026-07-13. Orca Whirlpools + Raydium CLMM = ticks/CLMM; Meteora DLMM = bins (zero slippage in active bin, position=NFT, dominates memecoin LP). ⚠️ Raydium-Stable ends in 'h' not 'P' (the 'P' variant is NOT-FOUND on-chain — verify before trusting a copied id). For swaps prefer the Jupiter aggregator; go direct for LP or a specific pool. See solana_dex_amms.

addresses.name: Monad core (chain 143)
addresses.note: All live-verified 2026-07-13 (chainId 143, ~400ms blocks, keyless rpc.monad.xyz, client Monad/0.14.5). WMON symbol/decimals(18) checked; USDC/USDT0 are 6 decimals (verified — NOT 18). Uniswap-v3 SwapRouter02 cross-verified: router.factory()→V3 factory AND router.WETH9()→WMON. Native USDC via CCTP v2 domain 15. Kuru CLOB / Uniswap V4 lead volume but addresses NOT pinned (verify before use). See monad_playbook.

addresses.name: Berachain core (chain 80094)
addresses.note: All live-verified 2026-07-13 (chainId 80094, ~2s blocks, keyless RPC rpc.berachain.com). WBERA/HONEY/BGT/iBGT symbol()+decimals() checked — ⚠️ HONEY has 18 decimals, not 6. BEX = Balancer-v2 fork (Vault-centric, WETH()→WBERA cross-verified), NOT CrocSwap anymore. Kodiak = Uniswap-v3 fork (router.factory()/WETH9() cross-verified). BGT soulbound (delegate via BeraChef, redeem 1:1→BERA, or hold liquid iBGT). PoL Reward Vaults via reward_vault_factory. See berachain_playbook.

addresses.name: Pendle (yield tokenization) — Ethereum
addresses.note: Router V4 = the tx.to that Pendle's Convert API returns for every swap/LP route (live-verified 2026-07-15: API-returned + eth_getCode has code). Per-market PT/YT/SY addresses are DYNAMIC — resolve them from /v1/{chainId}/markets/active per trade, never pin them. See pendle_yield_tokenization.

addresses.name: Delegation registries (delegate.xyz)
addresses.note: v2 = CREATE2 singleton, SAME address on 29+ EVM chains — bytecode verified identical on Ethereum/Base/Optimism/Arbitrum/Polygon 2026-07-15; zkSync-stack chains (Abstract/Era/Treasure) use the separate 0x…d797 address (the canonical one has NO code there). v1 (EIP-5639, 2022) read-only legacy. Pure attestation — a delegation grants zero token control. Keyless API api.delegate.xyz. See wallet_delegation.

addresses.name: SparkLend (Aave-v3 fork) — Ethereum
addresses.note: Aave-v3 fork (live-verified 2026-07-13: 18 reserves, ADDRESSES_PROVIDER cross-checked). supply/borrow/withdraw/repay with variable rate only (interestRateMode=2). NOT the same as Spark Savings (sUSDS/sDAI — see sky_usds_savings). See morpho_markets_vaults for disambiguation.

addresses.name: Gasless-payment stablecoins (EIP-3009 / EIP-2612)
addresses.note: All live-verified 2026-07-13 (6 decimals unless noted). EIP-712 domain VERSION differs per token and a wrong one reverts: USDC='2', PYUSD ('PayPal USD')='1', USDG ('Global Dollar')='1', USDe='1'. USDC/PYUSD/USDG support both EIP-3009 (transferWithAuthorization) + EIP-2612 (permit); USDe/USDS = permit only; USDT = NEITHER (needs Permit2 0x000000000022D473030F116dDEE9F6B43aC78BA3). ⚠️ Solana PYUSD/USDG are Token-2022 mints. See stablecoin_payment_rails.

addresses.name: BNB Chain DeFi core (chain 56)
addresses.note: Factory + WBNB cryptographically derived from the Router (factory()/WETH()) — beware: a near-identical FAKE WBNB address (same first 21 hex chars) circulates in LLM training data. 2.62M v2 pairs live. v3/Infinity addresses: docs.pancakeswap.finance. See bnb_chain_playbook.

addresses.name: Cronos DeFi core (chain 25)
addresses.note: Live-verified (23,463 pairs; WCRO symbol()-checked). Block time live-measured 0.40s — ignore stale '5s' training data. See cronos_playbook.

addresses.name: Robinhood Chain DeFi core (chain 4663)
addresses.note: All live-verified (factory derived from NPM.factory(); WETH via symbol()). ⚠️ Uniswap v3 and WETH are NOT at their canonical mainnet addresses here — only Permit2 is. See robinhood_chain_playbook guide.

addresses.name: Ethena (USDe / sUSDe) — Ethereum
addresses.note: Both symbol()-verified; sUSDe.asset()==USDe cross-verified (standard ERC-4626). Yield = perp funding + staking, variable and can go negative — see ethena_usde_mechanics guide.

addresses.name: Tokenized treasuries (RWA) — Ethereum
addresses.note: All live-verified via symbol()/name() (BUIDL = 'BlackRock USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund'). BUIDL/OUSG are KYC-whitelisted — transfer() reverts for non-whitelisted addresses despite the ERC-20 ABI. See tokenized_treasuries guide.

addresses.name: OP-Stack predeploys (Base, OP Mainnet, Zora, Mode …)
addresses.note: Identical on every OP-Stack chain. GasPriceOracle.getL1Fee(rawTx) prices the L1 data fee that eth_estimateGas misses (live-verified on Base); L1Block.number() reads the L1 block the L2 sees. See opstack_l2_fees guide.

addresses.name: EigenLayer core (restaking) — Ethereum
addresses.note: All proxies (live-verified; stETH-Strategy.underlyingToken() cross-checked = Lido stETH). LRTs: weETH 0xCd5fE23C85820F7B72D0926FC9b05b43E359b7ee, ezETH 0xbf5495Efe5DB9ce00f80364C8B423567e58d2110 (symbols live-verified). See restaking_eigenlayer guide for the stacked-risk model.

addresses.name: Balancer Vaults (v2 + v3)
addresses.note: v2 Vault holds ALL v2 pool liquidity behind one contract (live-verified byte-identical on Ethereum/Arbitrum/Base). poolId = 32 bytes (pool address + specialization + nonce); getPoolTokens(poolId) → tokens/balances; flash loans currently 0-fee. v3 Vault (live-verified, Ethereum) is interacted with via its Router, pools are plain contract addresses. See balancer_swaps guide.

addresses.name: Aave v3 Pool (lending) — Ethereum

errors.pattern: nonce too low / replacement transaction underpriced
errors.cause: Reusing a mined nonce, or resubmitting with the same nonce without raising fees.
errors.fix: Fetch eth_getTransactionCount(address, 'pending') fresh; to replace a stuck tx, resend the SAME nonce with maxFeePerGas AND maxPriorityFeePerGas raised >=10%.

errors.pattern: insufficient funds for gas * price + value
errors.cause: Balance < value + gasLimit*maxFeePerGas (the MAX fee is reserved upfront, not the actual fee).
errors.fix: Lower value or maxFeePerGas, or top up. Remember L1 data fees on L2s (Base/OP/Arbitrum) are charged on top.

errors.cause: Require/custom error in the contract; raw RPCs often swallow the reason.
errors.fix: Dry-run it with this server's `simulate` tool — it decodes revert reasons and custom errors (4byte fallback) before you sign.

errors.pattern: ERC20: transfer amount exceeds allowance / TRANSFER_FROM_FAILED
errors.cause: Spender allowance too low (or token uses non-standard return values).
errors.fix: Check allowance first (portfolio tool: allowance action), then approve the exact spender. Some tokens (USDT) require approving 0 before changing a non-zero allowance.

errors.pattern: max fee per gas less than block base fee
errors.fix: Re-quote EIP-1559 fees (profitability tool) and set maxFeePerGas ~= 2*baseFee + priorityFee headroom.

errors.pattern: gas required exceeds allowance / out of gas
errors.cause: eth_estimateGas failed (the call reverts) or the gasLimit was set below the estimate.
errors.fix: If estimateGas itself reverts, the tx would fail — simulate first. Otherwise add 10-20% buffer on the estimate.

errors.pattern: Solana: Blockhash not found / block height exceeded
errors.cause: Transaction used an expired recent blockhash (~60-90s validity).
errors.fix: Fetch a fresh blockhash right before signing; for congested periods add a priority fee (ComputeBudgetProgram.setComputeUnitPrice).

errors.cause: Account creation (e.g. an ATA, ~0.002 SOL) needs rent-exempt lamports the payer doesn't have.
errors.fix: Fund the payer with enough SOL for rent + fees; creating token accounts and pump.fun launches (~0.009 SOL rent) are NOT free.

errors.cause: Most commonly: insufficient token/lamport balance inside the invoked program.
errors.fix: Check token balance AND that the source ATA exists; decode program-specific errors from the program's IDL when available.

errors.pattern: eth_getLogs: range/limit errors on public RPCs
errors.cause: Free tiers cap block ranges hard (or require archive access).
errors.fix: Chunk requests (drpc.org free allows <=10k blocks; many others 500-2k; publicnode may require an archive token entirely). Filter by topic0 and address to shrink responses.

errors.pattern: Bitcoin: dust output / min relay fee not met
errors.cause: An output below the dust limit (~546 sats for P2PKH, ~294 for P2WPKH) or a tx paying under ~1 sat/vB is rejected by relay policy.
errors.fix: Merge tiny outputs into change, and set the feerate from mempool.space /v1/fees/recommended (sat/vB × estimated vsize).

errors.cause: An input UTXO was already spent (race with another tx, e.g. an RBF replacement) or never existed.
errors.fix: Re-fetch UTXOs (GET /address/{addr}/utxo) right before building; if you replaced a tx via RBF, the old inputs are gone.
Exponential backoff + failover to the next public endpoint (this server's chain registry ships multiple RPCs per chain). Cache aggressively.

errors.cause: The Reservoir NFT-aggregator API was shut down (team pivoted to Relay). Old tutorials and LLM training data still recommend it heavily.
errors.fix: Use OpenSea REST v2 (free key WITHOUT signup — see endpoints) or Magic Eden for NFT market data. Same graveyard: Hiro Ordinals API (410 Gone) → ordinals.com JSON / Ordiscan.

gotchas.detail: publicnode rejects it without an archive token; drpc.org free caps ranges at 10,000 blocks; ankr/merkle need keys. Chunk by block range and always filter by address + topic0.

Some public RPCs return data that makes high-level decoders throw (e.g. 'number not in safe integer range'). Fall back to a raw eth_call ({to, data} with the 4-byte selector + padded args) and decode the hex manually.
If it reverts, your tx would revert — treat estimate failures as a pre-flight rejection, not an RPC bug. Simulate with the exact from/value/state you will sign with.
Not all public endpoints allow batched arrays; some silently cap batch size. Multicall3 via a single eth_call is the more portable way to batch reads.
On OP-Stack + Arbitrum the L1 data fee is charged on top of the L2 execution fee; eth_estimateGas alone understates total cost. Use fee-estimation endpoints (or this server's profitability tool).
MCP servers answer 406 unless the client sends BOTH application/json and text/event-stream in Accept. When building MCP clients, always set: Accept: application/json, text/event-stream.
